Title: Ex-CEO acquitted of two abuse of power charges in Sabah court
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: A former company chief executive officer charged with two counts of abuse of power has been acquitted and discharged by the Special Corruption Court here today.
Sessions Court judge Jason Juga freed Sulaiman Mohd Noor, 59, without calling him to enter his defence after ruling that the prosecution had failed to establish a prima facie case.
Sulaiman had, on Feb 4, 2022, pleaded not guilty to both charges. https://www.nst.com.my/news/crime-courts/2022/02/768706/former-ceo-charged-power-abuse-involving-more-rm2-million
On the first charge, Sulaiman, in his capacity as a public officer and CEO of YBS Tenaga Sdn Bhd, was accused of using his position for gratification by appointing Syarikat Pemborong Tambun Jaya to carry out Mechanical and Electrical Works (33KV Transmission Line) valued at RM2,275,115.92.
On the second charge, he was alleged to have appointed the same company to undertake maintenance works for the existing road to Kampung Minitampak 3 (Chainage KM2.00 to KM8.00) worth RM313,963.40.
Both charges stated that Sulaiman was a co-owner of Syarikat Pemborong Tambun Jaya, which was a subcontractor for YBS Tenaga Sdn Bhd for the said projects.
The alleged offences took place on March 12, 2018, and Oct 2, 2017, respectively, at the YBS Tenaga Sdn Bhd office here.
The charges were framed under Section 23(1) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act 2009, punishable under Section 24(1) of the same Act, which provides for a maximum jail term of 20 years and a fine of five times the value of the gratification or RM10,000, whichever is higher, upon conviction.
Lawyer Zahir Shah represented Sulaiman, while MACC deputy public prosecutor Nurul Izzati Sapifee conducted the prosecution.
The prosecution called 14 witnesses during the trial, which began on Feb 5, 2024.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor assembly passes bill allowing appointment of non-elected assemblymen
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: The Johor State Legislative Assembly today passed a bill to amend the state enactment, allowing for the appointment of up to five non-elected state assemblymen to strengthen the composition and functions of the legislative institution.
The bill to amend Part Two, Clause 15 of the Johor Constitution 1985 was passed after securing more than two-thirds support, with 40 assemblymen in favour and 16 opposing it.
Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, when tabling the bill at Bangunan Sultan Ismail here, said the proposed appointments were aimed at broadening participation in the State Legislative Assembly, especially through the involvement of individuals with expertise and experience to enhance debate and the policymaking process.
"In the context of Johor's rapid development and the influx of new investments, the need to expand the range of views in the State Legislative Assembly is becoming increasingly important.
"The government is confident that this proposal will enhance the State Legislative Assembly's capacity to assess and make decisions in a more comprehensive and effective manner," he said.
Onn Hafiz said the amendment was implemented in accordance with due legal procedures, including the requirement that it receive the support of not less than two-thirds of the assemblymen.
"This approach has also been adopted and practised in several other states, such as Pahang, Terengganu and Sabah," said the Machap assemblyman.
Onn Hafiz said the bill, among others, included a second clause which seeks to allow the appointment of no more than five non-elected assemblymen.
He said the amendment could see the number of assemblymen increase from 56 to 61.
"These members (assemblymen) did not go through the election process but were instead appointed to complement the existing composition of the State Legislative Assembly," he said.
Onn Hafiz said the third clause introduced Clauses 15A, 15B and 15C, which provide for appointments to be made through a motion in the State Legislative Assembly, subject to the consent of the Sultan.
Anuar Abdul Manap (BN-Pemanis), during the debate on the bill, said the appointment of non-elected assemblymen would not only increase the number of seats, but also improve the quality of services delivered to the people.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar arrives in Cebu for Asean leaders' summit
Article:
CEBU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim arrived in Cebu, Philippines, today to attend the 48th Asean Summit and related meetings, which will continue until tomorrow.
The special aircraft carrying the prime minister landed at Mactan-Benito Ebuen Air Base at about 1.50pm.
Upon arrival, he was received by Philippine Cabinet Secretary and Minister-in-Attendance Sharin S. Garin; Cebu Governor Pamela Baricuatro; and Lapu-Lapu City Mayor Cynthia King Chan.
Also present to welcome Anwar was Malaysia's Ambassador to the Philippines Datuk Abdul Malik Melvin Castelino Anthony.
In addition to attending the 48th Asean Summit, Anwar is also scheduled to participate in the Brunei Darussalam–Indonesia–Malaysia–Philippines East Asean Growth Area (BIMP-EAGA) Special Summit this afternoon.
During his stay in Cebu, Anwar is also expected to hold several bilateral meetings with Asean leaders.
Malaysia's participation in this summit reflects the country's commitment to strengthening Asean centrality, regional unity and the region's economic and geopolitical resilience amid an increasingly challenging global environment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Meeting on Kampung Bohol flood retention pond set for May 12, says Hannah Yeoh
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A meeting will be held on May 12 to discuss the status of the Kampung Bohol flood retention pond following concerns over flash floods in the area.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the meeting would be convened by Federal Territories Department director-general Datuk Azmi Mohd Zain.
She said the discussion would also involve the Kuala Lumpur Land and Mines Office.
"For Kampung Bohol, we will call a meeting and it will involve the Land Office for discussions.
"Residents informed us that the rain yesterday was quite heavy. Several areas in Kuala Lumpur experienced flash floods, but the water subsided shortly afterwards," she said at a press conference after announcing the gazetting of four green and open spaces at a recreational park in Taman Lemak today.
Yeoh was responding to questions on the flash floods that hit several parts of the Klang Valley, including Kuala Lumpur, yesterday.
Also present were Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud and Cheras member of parliament Tan Kok Wai.
The Kampung Bohol flood retention pond is a key component of the local drainage system, located next to Sungai Kuyoh and the Shah Alam Expressway (Kesas).
It serves several surrounding areas, including Kampung Muhibbah in Kuala Lumpur, Taman Kinrara in Selangor, and developments along the Kesas corridor.
The pond had previously been
identified for upgrading works aimed at increasing its capacity and reducing flooding
in nearby areas.
The upgrading works were to be carried out by a private developer as part of a housing project tender announced in 2024, and were to be completed before other development on the associated plots could proceed.
However, questions remain over the project's status and timeline, with no clear public update on its completion as of 2026.
On the gazetting of green and open spaces, Yeoh said the latest announcement came less than three weeks after 45 spaces in Kuala Lumpur were gazetted on April 15, following the gazetting of 494 areas on Feb 5.
The four newly gazetted sites comprise two open spaces at Jalan Damai Puspa, Alam Damai, as well as a playground and community hall at Persiaran Lemak in Cheras.
"With this latest addition, the total number of green and open spaces gazetted has increased to 543 areas," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Passengers on hantavirus-stricken ship sway between fear, boredom
Article:
IT was billed as an Atlantic odyssey to some of the most remote islands in the world.
Instead, the cruise on the hantavirus-stricken MV Hondius was left stranded off Cape Verde with passengers in their cabins, medics in protective suits tending to the sick and the ship's operator seeking a safe port.
The outbreak has left three dead and eight confirmed or suspected cases linked to the Dutch-flagged expedition ship.
Passengers, some of whom have been aboard since March 20, have reported moods swinging between fear and boredom: empty lounges, quiet decks, hot drinks, face masks, medical checks, and the uncertainty of not knowing when and how their journey will end.
On Wednesday, specialist teams evacuated three people as the vessel prepared to extend its voyage to Spain's Canary Islands with the consent of local authorities.
After four days stationed off the West African archipelago, the ship set sail late Wednesday toward the Canary island of Tenerife, where around 150 remaining passengers and crew may finally disembark under medical supervision. It is not clear whether they will be quarantined on arrival.
Cruise operator Oceanwide Expeditions has told passengers to follow "isolation measures, hygiene protocols and medical monitoring."
Martin Kriz, a Swedish doctor who has served aboard the Hondius, said that means taking meals in cramped 4-person cabins.
"It's quite a small space," he told Reuters.
But passengers say conditions were not bad. Passenger Kasem Hato said the mood was calm.
"People are taking the situation seriously but without any panic, trying to keep social distancing and wearing masks to be safe," he told Reuters.
"Our days have been close to normal, just waiting for authorities to find a solution, but morale on the ship is high and we're keeping ourselves busy with reading, watching movies, having hot drinks, and that kind of things."
One clip showed the Hondius's wood-paneled lounge looking clean, expensive and empty. Armchairs and couches sat grouped on colourful carpets, with the sea visible through windows.
Images posted on social media and reviewed by Reuters showed large bags of supplies being delivered to the ship's deck by workers in scrubs and masks.
The scene marked a stark contrast with photos of spectacular Antarctic views passengers shared earlier in the voyage.
Jake Rosmarin of the United States became one of the best-known faces on the ship after posting an emotional video from his cabin on Instagram fretting over the uncertainty passengers faced.
Later he struck a calmer note.
"I'm feeling well, getting some fresh air, and continuing to be well fed and taken care of by the crew," he said in a follow-up post.
Turkish YouTuber Ruhi Cenet, who departed the ship at Saint Helena April 24, was more critical.
He said after the first passenger died on April 11, passengers were told he was not infectious, so they continued socialising and taking meals together.
With quicker isolation measures "I think this problem could have been small before spreading too much," he told Reuters from Istanbul.
Oceanwide said in a statement on Wednesday that information relayed by the ship's captain was accurate at the time, and he had followed proper health and safety standards following a death at sea.
Belgian passenger Helene Goessaert told Belgian broadcaster VRT the atmosphere was "relatively good", with fresh fruit and vegetables still coming aboard. She praised the crew.
"We are all in the same boat, literally," she said.
Goessaert said that after rough waters earlier in the trip, passengers are not easily shaken.
"I think the people on board can take a few lumps," she said.
* The writers are from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Emirates reports record 2025/26 group revenue, up 3pct on year
Article:
DUBAI: Emirates Group posted record group revenue of 150.5 billion dirhams (US$40.98 billion) in the 2025/26 financial year, up 3 per cent from the previous year, it said in a statement.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Shell quarterly net profit jumps 19pct as war fuels oil prices
Article:
LONDON: British energy giant Shell announced Thursday a 19-per cent increase in net profits for the first quarter as the Middle East war sent oil and gas prices soaring in volatile trading.
Profit after tax increased to US$5.69 billion in the January-March period from US$4.78 billion in the first quarter of 2025, Shell said in an earnings statement.
"Shell delivered strong results enabled by our relentless focus on operational performance in a quarter marked by unprecedented disruption in global energy markets," said chief executive Wael Sawan.
In addition to benefiting from soaring prices at the pump caused by the Iran war that began on Feb 28, energy majors have profited from their trades as oil and gas futures swung between big gains and losses on the war's latest headlines.
Shell's total revenue was stable at around US$70 billion in the first quarter year-on-year but rose from US$66.7 billion in the fourth quarter of 2025.
Shell's gas production was meanwhile impacted after the world's largest liquefied natural gas hub, Ras Laffan in northern Qatar, suffered significant damage in the war.
"The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has resulted in production shutdowns and export constraints," Shell said in its earnings release.
"Since the start of the conflict, commodity prices and refining margins have been highly volatile," it added.
Alongside the results, Shell boosted its shareholders by announcing it would repurchase US$3 billion worth of its shares as well as hiking its dividend.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Invasive suckerfish choke Jakarta rivers in mounting environmental crisis
Article:
JAKARTA: Mounds of slimey carcasses pile up on a riverbank in Jakarta where authorities are fighting an uphill battle against a fast-breeding invasive fish flourishing in Java island's heavily polluted waterways.
The sapu-sapu, a sucking fish introduced from South America decades ago to keep Southeast Asian aquariums algae-free, has made a home in Javan rivers, many after being thrown out for outgrowing their tanks.
Unlike in the Amazon River where it has natural predators, the sapu-sapu quickly took over in Java, outbreeding indigenous freshwater fish, eating their eggs and outcompeting them for food.
Residents and campaigners have complained about the stinky, unsightly pileups of fishy remains, and the potential health risk for those who consume snacks made from polluted sapu-sapu stock.
Invasive species are spreading ever faster across the globe and cost well over US$400 billion a year in damages and lost income, according to a 2023 United Nations assessment.
Surprisingly adept at living in polluted water, the sapu-sapu – also known as suckermouth catfish, janitor fish or pleco – can grow to half a metre (20 inches) in length and a female can lay thousands of eggs every year.
"That there are thousands of (sapu-sapu) fish in some of these river bodies, where, you know, the rivers are like dark black, almost smelling like rotten eggs... is completely crazy," clean river campaigner Gary Bencheghib told AFP.
Killing sapu-sapu is not addressing the real problem, he added in a phone interview, nearly halfway through a 1,200-kilometre (746-mile) run from Bali to Jakarta to raise money for river cleanups and having witnessed the crisis at several stops along the way.
The real solution is cleaning up "the waste it lives off... that you find in these polluted waters," said Bencheghib, co-founder of the Sungai Watch NGO.
- 'A DANGEROUS SNACK' -
More than half of Indonesia's rivers are heavily polluted, and two of the country's major river systems are among the dirtiest in the world, according to data from the UN Environment Programme.
Nationwide, only about 7.4 percent of municipal wastewater is safely collected and treated.
Greater Jakarta, a water-stressed metropolis of 42 million people, is particularly affected.
But sapu-sapu are able to live in oxygen-poor and polluted waters, digging holes into riverbanks to lay their eggs and weakening walls that have been known to collapse as a result.
In recent weeks, Jakarta has embarked on a killing campaign involving residents, sanitation workers, fisheries ministry workers and even soldiers.
In two weeks, about 5.3 tonnes of the fish have been removed from rivers in South Jakarta alone, mayor Muhammad Anwar told AFP while overseeing an operation at Babakan Lake this week.
The sapu-sapu are caught in nets, separated from indigenous fish that are thrown back, then decapitated to make sure they are dead, and buried in mass graves.
Some activists have criticised the procedure as wasteful, arguing the skin could be used for fish leather products and the rest processed into fertiliser or animal feed.
But others say the fish are too contaminated to be useful, and definitely not fit for human consumption.
"It contains... heavy metals and is dangerous to humans," Anwar said.
Tests conducted for scientific studies have found traces of lead and mercury as well as E.coli bacteria beyond safe levels in sapu-sapu.
"In some places, it is consumed as a dangerous snack called siomay (steamed fish dumpling) and pempek (fish cakes)," the mayor said.
"Please be careful when buying fish cakes. Don't be led by the (cheap) price."--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank Negara holds OPR steady at 2.75pct
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia has kept the overnight policy rate (OPR) unchanged at 2.75 per cent, maintaining the level set in July 2025.
In its third Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting for the year, the committee considers the monetary policy stance to be appropriate and consistent with the outlook of continued price stability and sustainable economic growth.
"The MPC will remain vigilant to ongoing developments and assess the balance of risks surrounding the outlook for domestic inflation and growth," it said in a statement.
Bank Negara said global growth remained resilient in the first quarter of 2026, supported mainly by sustained domestic demand and continued global tech expansion.
However, sharp increases in energy and commodity prices as well as supply chain disruptions from the Middle East conflict are beginning to weigh on the global growth momentum.
The central bank said the risks to global growth remain elevated stemming from the uncertainties surrounding the length and severity of the conflict, tighter global financial conditions and concerns over valuations in financial markets.
Meanwhile, the upside potential includes de-escalation of the conflict leading to improving supply chain conditions, stronger tech spending and pro growth policy measures in key economies.
For Malaysia, latest indicators point towards continued growth momentum in the first quarter, driven by sustained domestic demand and strong export performance.
"Moving forward, uncertainties surrounding the duration and severity of the Middle East conflict will affect the outlook of domestic growth and inflation.
"However, Malaysia's strong fundamentals will continue to underpin the economy's resilience," it said.
Bank Negara added that employment, wage growth and policy measures will remain supportive of household spending.
It said investment activity will be driven by the progress of multi-year projects in both the private and public sectors, implementation of new smaller-scale public projects, continued high realisation of approved investments, as well as the ongoing implementation of national master plans.
The external sector will benefit from continued strength in electrical and electronics (E&E) exports, while tourist spending will be sustained albeit at a more moderate pace.
This growth outlook remains subject to downside risks from a prolonged conflict in the Middle East and lower commodity production.
Meanwhile, upside potential to growth could arise from the de-escalation of the conflict, stronger demand for E&E goods and higher tourism activity.
The central bank warned that higher global commodity prices arising from the Middle East conflict are expected to raise domestic cost pressures, causing inflation to edge higher.
However, the impact on both headline and core inflation in 2026 is expected to remain contained, reflecting domestic policy measures and stable demand conditions, which will mitigate the pass-through of external cost pressures to domestic prices.
"The MPC acknowledges the uncertainties from the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The impact to the global and Malaysian economy will depend on how these developments evolve.
"The MPC will remain vigilant to ongoing developments and assess the balance of risks surrounding the outlook for domestic inflation and growth," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SD Guthrie cautious on outlook after Q1 earnings eases to RM560mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: SD Guthrie Bhd, one of the world's largest palm oil producers, is cautious on its outlook for the year, after reporting a slightly lower first-quarter net profit on weaker crude palm oil and palm kernel price.
Its net profit fell 1.2 per cent to RM560 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 from RM567 million a year ago.
SD Guthrie's revenue eased 2.7 per cent to RM4.69 billion from RM4.82 billion previously, while its earnings per share came in lower at 8.10 sen compared to 8.20 sen.
The group's upstream segment recorded a profit before interest and tax (PBIT) of RM529 million.
The segment was affected by lower realised crude palm oil (CPO) and palm kernel prices, which fell 10 per cent and eight per cent respectively from a year earlier.
SD Guthrie International, the group's downstream segment, posted a 43 per cent year-on-year increase in PBIT to RM109 million, driven by higher sales volumes, improved margins in Asia Pacific and Europe and stronger contributions from joint ventures.
The industrial development segment posted RM160 million in PBIT for Q1 FY26, mainly from an outright third-party land sale.
Chairman Tan Sri Dr Nik Norzrul Thani Nik Hassan Thani said amid the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, SD Guthrie's diversified portfolio delivered a resilient performance to record a good start to the year.
"The group's new leadership offers the stability on the back of a challenging macroeconomic backdrop and will continue to bring renewed momentum to fuel the group's growth ambitions," he said in a separate statement.
Group managing director Mohd Haris Mohd Arshad said it would continue to improve profitability across its diversified portfolio for the remainder of the year.
The group remains cautious on its outlook for FY26 due to volatile market conditions and policy uncertainties
"While market conditions remain unpredictable, the team is focused on what we can control namely productivity, quality and costs, to sustain and build on last year's strong performance.
"While SD Guthrie is cautious in the challenging operating environment, we are well positioned to look ahead with confidence, leveraging on our strong balance sheet, sound strategies and effective execution," he said.
SD Guthrie expects CPO prices to remain supported in the near term due to weather-related supply risks and the conflict in the Middle East.
"The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has disrupted global supply chains and raised energy and fuel prices, increasing costs related to logistics, fertilisers and energy.
"The expansion of regional biodiesel mandates is also expected to support CPO prices, although uncertainties remain over the implementation of these mandates," it noted.
SD Guthrie said it will continue focusing on yield improvement and cost management in its upstream operations to manage market volatility.
The downstream segment will focus on protecting margins through its integrated operations and geographic diversification.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Most EU citizens do not see US as trustworthy partner, study finds
Article:
BERLIN, (Germany): A majority of citizens in the European Union do not see the United States as a trustworthy partner, according to a study based on surveys in all 27 member states.
Donald Trump's presidency has left deep marks, the Bertelsmann Foundation said in an analysis. More than 18,000 adults aged 18 to 69 were surveyed representatively by the research institute Nira Data in March.
According to the study, 58 per cent of EU citizens said the US was not a trustworthy partner. In Germany, the figure was even higher at 73 per cent, study author Florian Kommer told dpa.
Only 31 per cent of those surveyed considered the US to be the EU's most important partner.
In September 2024, the figure had stood at 51 per cent, marking a decline of 20 percentage points.
Almost three in four respondents, or 73 per cent, said Europe should "go its own way" after decades of close ties with the US.
In late 2024, 63 per cent of respondents held that view.
The foundation concluded that "a polarising US president and increasing geopolitical tensions have shifted public opinion in Europe towards greater independence."
At the same time, the study suggested that China is not gaining importance among EU respondents as a possible alternative partner, with scepticism towards Beijing remaining high.
The United Kingdom and Canada are seen as playing a greater role as strategic partners, according to the study.
Support for the NATO defence alliance remains strong, with 63 per cent of respondents continuing to see it as a central pillar of security. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor among states with highest household income levels since 2022
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: Household income in Johor has increased since the current state administration took office in 2022, placing the state among the highest in Malaysia in terms of household income levels.
State Investment, Trade, Consumer Affairs and Human Resources Committee chairman Lee Ting Han said data from the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) showed that Johor's median household income in 2022 stood at about RM6,879 per month, while average household income was around RM8,517.
Based on the latest 2024/2025 data, the average household income in the state has since risen to about RM9,484 per month, while the median household income increased to around RM7,712 per month.
"This shows that economic growth and investment inflows into Johor are not merely on paper, but are translating into higher incomes for the people," he said during the question-and-answer session at the Johor State Legislative Assembly sitting here today.
Lee (BN-Paloh) was responding to questions from Ee Chin Li (DAP-Tangkak), Nor Rashidah Ramli (BN-Parit Raja), Hahasrin Hashim (BN-Panti), Liew Chin Tong (DAP-Perling) and Liow Cai Tung (DAP-Johor Jaya) on the impact of foreign investments on the rakyat and initiatives to support the capacity development of local companies.
Lee also said DOSM's Labour Force Survey showed that Johor's unemployment rate remained stable at 2.5 per cent as of end-2025, compared with the national average of 2.9 per cent.
He said Johor's economy is not solely dependent on foreign investment. The state recorded RM49.5 billion in domestic direct investment (DDI) and RM60.5 billion in foreign direct investment (FDI) last year.
"This demonstrates that Johor's economic growth today is driven jointly by the confidence of both domestic and international investors.
"This is important because a healthy economy requires both to grow in tandem and complement each other," said Lee. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: EU countries, lawmakers clinch provisional deal on watered-down AI rules
Article:
BRUSSELS: EU countries and European Parliament lawmakers on Thursday agreed to watered-down landmark artificial intelligence rules, including delaying their implementation, in a move which critics say shows Europe caving to Big Tech.
The tentative agreement, which needs to be formally endorsed by EU governments and the European Parliament in the coming months, came after nine hours of negotiations.
"Today's agreement on the AI Act significantly supports our companies by reducing recurring administrative costs," Marilena Raouna, Cyprus's deputy minister for European affairs, said in a statement.
Cyprus currently holds the rotating EU Council presidency.
The changes to the AI Act, which entered into force in August 2024 with key elements to be enforced in stages, are part of the European Commission's push to simplify a slew of new digital rules.
The simplification drive came after businesses complained about overlapping regulations and red tape that hamper their ability to compete with US and Asian rivals.
EU governments and lawmakers agreed to delay rules on high-risk AI systems such as those involving biometrics or related to critical infrastructure and law enforcement to Dec 2, 2027 from a previous deadline of Aug 2 this year.
They also agreed to exclude machinery from the AI Act as it is already subject to sectoral rules, ceding to pressure from businesses.
There was also agreement on a ban on AI practices which create unauthorised sexually explicit images, a move responding to such content generated by Elon Musk's xAI ​chatbot Grok on ​X and sexually intimate deepfakes produced by Grok.
The ban will apply from Dec 2.
"By the end of this year everyone, but especially women and girls will be safe from horrific nudifier apps being widely available on the EU market.
"Today we put a clear end to this kind of violence against people and children," said Dutch lawmaker ​Kim van Sparrentak.
Mandatory watermarking of AI generated output will apply from Dec 2.
The AI rules, which were triggered by concerns about the impact of the technology on children, workers, companies and cybersecurity, are still considered the strictest in the world even after the changes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No Umno proposal to replace Aminuddin as Negri MB, says Zahid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: There is no proposal from Umno to replace Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun as Negri Sembilan menteri besar, says party president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
"No, there is no such proposal," Zahid told reporters on the sidelines of the launch of the National Social Policy Action Plan 2026–2030.
Zahid was responding to a question on whether there was a need to replace the Negri Sembilan menteri besar after Umno's 14 assemblymen said they would not reverse their decision to withdraw support for Aminuddin.
Previously, Umno and Pakatan Harapan (PH) said they would maintain their cooperation in Negri Sembilan.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sri Lanka arrests 231 foreigners in latest crackdown on cyberscams
Article:
COLOMBO: Sri Lanka police have arrested 231 foreign nationals, mostly Chinese and Vietnamese, following raids on suspected cyberscam centres in the capital since the weekend, officials said Thursday.
Crime groups internationally have carried out online cons, defrauding victims of tens of billions of dollars annually, often through cryptocurrency scams and phony romantic relationships.
In Sri Lanka, authorities cracked down on two suspected cybserscam centres on Sunday, arresting a total of 157 people.
An additional 74 people were arrested on Wednesday at a third location.
"They had entered the country on tourist visas and were illegally employed, while some had overstayed their visas," police spokesman Frederick Wootler said.
A Colombo magistrate ordered a forensic examination of computers and mobile phones seized from the alleged scam centre in Colombo's Kollupitiya area on Wednesday.
Computer and communications equipment found with the suspects arrested on Sunday had already been sent for analysis, officials said.
The arrests came a month after 152 foreign nationals, mostly Chinese, were detained for allegedly running a cyberscam operation out of a hotel in the island's northwest.
Immigration authorities arrested 135 Chinese men and women in March for allegedly running a similar scam operation. They have since been deported.
Beijing's embassy in Colombo said at the time that it was working closely with local authorities to prevent Chinese nationals from engaging in scam operations in Sri Lanka.
The embassy said Sri Lanka's telecommunications infrastructure, favourable geographical location and relatively lenient visa policies had encouraged fraud gangs to move to the South Asian nation.
In 2024, Sri Lankan authorities detained 230 Chinese nationals and 200 Indians accused of operating cybercrime centres in various parts of the island.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Higher shipping costs, longer lead times adversely hit Malaysian manufacturers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Freight and logistics disruptions continue to weigh on Malaysian manufacturers amid Middle East crisis, with companies facing sharply higher shipping costs and longer delivery times.
The Federation of Malaysian Manufacturing (FMM) said 87 per cent of survey respondents reported freight costs had increased since Feb 28, while half of them experienced increases of between 20 and 50 per cent above pre-conflict levels.
FMM president Jacob Lee said rerouting of vessels via the Cape of Good Hope has significantly extended transit times to Europe.
He said routes that previously took between 25 and 30 days are now taking between 35 and 45 days, while some shipments are delayed by more than 30 additional days.
Domestic logistics have also deteriorated, with 49 per cent of respondents reporting higher transport costs within Malaysia.
"The breakdown in haulage connectivity between Pasir Gudang and the Port of Tanjung Pelepas is specifically cited as a serious bottleneck. The root cause in many cases is diesel quota exhaustion.
"When hauliers exhaust their subsidised diesel allocation, operating costs rise sharply and less profitable routes are abandoned, with the cost falling on the manufacturer who cannot move goods to port on schedule," he said in a statement.
According to the survey, 37 per cent said shipping lines had imposed unregulated fuel adjustment surcharges, while 23 per cent were dealing with higher detention and demurrage charges.
"10 per cent have had export cargo returned, abandoned or redirected due to port access restrictions in the Gulf, with commercial losses per company ranging from several hundred thousand US dollars to over US$1 million," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCMC tightens QoS standards for TV, radio providers
Article:
CYBERJAYA: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has announced revised mandatory quality of service (QoS) standards for content applications service providers (CASPs) to strengthen customer protection and improve complaint resolution.
In a statement today, MCMC said the revised standards apply to all CASPs delivering television and radio content nationwide, including free-to-air television, subscription-based television and terrestrial radio stations, involving 34 active providers.
"The revised standards go beyond just ensuring services are up and running, placing stronger focus on how well services actually perform for consumers, including faster complaint resolution and more consistent service quality," the commission said.
MCMC said the updated framework places greater accountability on CASPs in areas such as service availability, performance monitoring and timely resolution of service-related issues.
The strengthened framework now covers both subscription and non-subscription content application services, while introducing enhanced reporting and governance requirements, clearer QoS benchmarks and stronger complaint resolution obligations.
Among the improvements are expanded timelines for resolving non-billing complaints, as well as stricter requirements on service responsiveness, record retention and performance transparency.
"These enhancements are intended to ensure that customer complaints are managed more effectively, service disruptions are addressed more promptly and issues are reasonably resolved and accepted by consumers," MCMC said.
Under the revised standards, CASPs must submit periodic QoS performance reports and maintain auditable records for at least two years.
MCMC said it may also conduct audits, sampling exercises and service observations, in addition to publishing performance outcomes to promote transparency.
The revised determination follows a public inquiry conducted between Nov 22, 2025 and Jan 31, 2026, involving industry stakeholders, service providers and members of the public.
The changes were implemented through the Commission Determination on the Mandatory Standards for QoS (Content Applications Services), replacing Determination No. 4 of 2002 with Determination No. 2 of 2026.
"Through this determination, MCMC reaffirms its commitment to ensuring a fair, reliable and customer-centric communications and multimedia ecosystem," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hunger deepening in Gaza as families survive on one meal a day: UN
Article:
WASHINGTON: The UN on Wednesday warned that hunger remains widespread and severe across the Gaza Strip, with millions struggling to access adequate food despite ongoing humanitarian efforts, Anadolu Ajansi reported.
Speaking to reporters, UN spokesman Stephane Dujarric cited data from the Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA), which estimates that 2.1 million people in Gaza are now confined to less than half of the territory, limiting access to farmland, food supplies, and essential services.
"People are unable to access parts of Gaza where land reserves and critical facilities, such as solid waste landfills, are present.
"They are also unable to travel abroad or access the West Bank, where services such as specialised health care are available to them.
"People who are allowed to exit as part of medical evacuations represent a tiny fraction of those who need services that are unavailable on a daily basis in Gaza," he said.
Citing the World Food Programme (WFP), Dujarric said more than 1.6 million people receive food parcels, hot meals, bread, or cash assistance each month.
In addition, humanitarian partners are providing roughly 1.1 million meals daily through more than 120 community kitchens across the Gaza Strip.
"Yet, more than six months since the declaration of a ceasefire, WFP said hunger hasn't disappeared, noting high malnutrition levels.
Many families still rely on food assistance to get by, as fresh food remains too expensive. One in five families eats only one meal every day," Dujarric said.
Responding to Anadolu's question on whether repeated warnings about insufficient aid are being heard, Dujarric said the organisation hopes its message is reaching beyond the press room — but acknowledged limited results.
"We hope that others besides you hear us. These are issues we've raised with the Israeli parties on a daily basis. We've also raised them with American counterparts. The bottom line is there hasn't been much positive movement on that.
"We don't control the system. We know we're on the ground. Our colleagues see with their own eyes exactly where the situation is. They know what's needed, and we're just not getting it," Dujarric said.
Israel launched a genocidal war on the Gaza Strip in October 2023, killing more than 72,000 Palestinians and injuring over 172,000, most of them women and children.
Despite a ceasefire agreement that has been in effect since last Oct 10, Israel has continued deadly attacks and a blockade on Gaza, killing 837 Palestinians and injuring 2,381 others, in addition to causing widespread destruction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Royal institution vital in ensuring integrity in state governance, says Selangor Umno
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Selangor Umno welcomes and supports the statement yesterday by the Selangor Council of the Royal Court regarding the role and responsibilities of the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, as a Constitutional Ruler.
State Umno liaison committee chairman Datuk Megat Zulkarnain Omardin said the statement was important in clarifying to the public that every decree, advice, reprimand and reminder issued by the Sultan of Selangor was in line with the spirit of Constitutional Monarchy and His Royal Highness' role as the state ruler, with priority given to the interests of the people and the stability of the state.
"The statement is very important in clarifying to the people that every decree, advice, reprimand and reminder issued by the Sultan of Selangor is in line with the spirit of Constitutional Monarchy and His Royal Highness' role as state ruler, prioritising the interests of the people and the stability of the state," he said in a statement today.
Megat Zulkarnain said all parties must respect the position and role of Sultan Sharafuddin in accordance with the Federal Constitution and the Laws of the Constitution of Selangor 1959, without politicising His Royal Highness' decrees and views.
He said the royal institution was the protector of the state, a symbol of unity among the people, and a pillar of stability that must be preserved and respected regardless of political affiliation.
According to him, the institution also plays an important role in ensuring the state administration is carried out responsibly, with integrity and in the best interests of the people.
He said Selangor Umno remained steadfast in defending the Selangor royal institution against any form of insult, slander, provocation or attempts to undermine the dignity of Sultan Sharafuddin.
"Previously, Selangor Umno had consistently defended the Sultan of Selangor's decree regarding the pig farming issue in the state, which certain parties attempted to distort, besides condemning the rude act of editing and insulting the image of the Sultan of Selangor, which clearly crossed the boundaries of decorum, culture and respect for the royal institution.
"In today's increasingly challenging political climate, Selangor Umno calls on all parties to be more mature, prudent and responsible when issuing statements related to the royal institution, and not to turn matters involving the Sultan of Selangor into narrow political polemics," he added.
Megat Zulkarnain added that all parties should focus on addressing issues affecting the people, strengthening state stability and ensuring effective administration in line with the spirit of Constitutional Monarchy practised in the country.
Yesterday, the Selangor Council of the Royal Court stressed that the Sultan of Selangor consistently
emphasised effective action
rather than empty promises in resolving issues affecting the people, in line with His Royal Highness' role as Head of State.
The council said any advice, reprimand and reminder by the Sultan of Selangor constituted important guidance from a Ruler to ensure the state administration was carried out responsibly.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: High political costs may undermine democracy, warns IDEAS
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Institute for Democracy and Economic Affairs (IDEAS) has warned that high political costs and the absence of caretaker government rules could undermine democratic fairness and public trust in Malaysia.
The independent think tank launched two policy papers raising concerns over opaque political financing and the absence of a comprehensive framework governing executive conduct during political transitions, the statement said.
The first paper, titled "How Costly are Political Parties? Exploring Political Expenses and Implications for Democracy", found that political financing in Malaysia remains expensive, uneven and lacking transparency, with newer parties and underrepresented groups facing significant financial barriers.
Co-author of the paper and IDEAS research assistant manager Syazwan Zainal said Malaysia remains among the few countries without regulations on political financing.
"While it is a fact that political parties around the world need money to operate effectively, Malaysia stands out as one of the very few countries that do not have regulations on political financing.
"However, a Political Financing Act is not a cure-all for rising political costs, but it would improve transparency, limit political donations and create a fairer funding system for parties," Syazwan said.
The second paper, "Guidance for Malaysia's Caretaker Government: Lessons from Four Commonwealth Jurisdictions", highlighted the absence of publicly accessible caretaker government guidelines during periods of political transition.
Its author Chang Xi Wo said clearer rules were needed amid increasingly frequent political transitions in Malaysia.
"With political transitions becoming more frequent in Malaysia, comprehensive caretaker government guidelines are needed to ensure fair elections, accountability, policy stability and public trust in democratic institutions.
"This is an important step for Malaysia's evolving multi-party democracy, especially as post-election governing coalition negotiations and mid-term realignments might become more common," he added.
IDEAS chief executive officer Aira Azhari called for the immediate enactment of a Political Financing Act, stronger disclosure and oversight mechanisms, and the publication of clear caretaker government guidelines.
"Fair competition and transparency are essential to a healthy democracy.
"Stronger political financing rules, alongside clear caretaker government guidelines, will reduce opportunities for abuse, improve accountability and help level the political playing field," she said.
"Together, these reforms strengthen trust in institutions and support better democratic outcomes," Aira added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Assistant warden arrested over teen's alleged sexual assault
Article:
GUA MUSANG: Police have arrested an assistant warden at a secondary school to assist in investigations into the alleged sexual assault of a 17-year-old male student late last month.
Gua Musang police chief Superintendent Sik Choon Foo said the 37-year-old suspect was detained yesterday following a report lodged by the victim.
"The student recounted the incident to a teacher, who then accompanied the victim to lodge a police report.
"The suspect was arrested on the same day and has been remanded for seven days starting today," Sik said.
Earlier, police obtained a remand order from the Magistrate's Court to detain the man for investigations under Section 14(d) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act.
The offence carries a jail term of up to 20 years and caning.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malayan Flour Mills expands into prepared food segment
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malayan Flour Mills Bhd (MFM) has expanded into the ready-to-eat (RTE) meals segment as part of its 60th anniversary initiatives, reflecting growing consumer demand for convenient food products.
The group launched a new range of microwaveable protein-based meals, marking a move further into prepared food solutions leveraging its existing poultry processing operations.
Executive deputy chairman and managing director Teh Wee Che said the expansion forms part of the company's long-term growth plans.
"The expansion reflects MFM's continued focus on supporting food security through resilient, safe and dependable food production," he said at the launch today.
The new RTE products are produced through the group's vertically integrated operations including Dindings Poultry Processing Sdn Bhd in Manjung, Perak.
The initial product lineup consists of four halal-certified microwaveable meals, including charcoal-grilled satay, Ayam Masak Merah, Ayam Kicap and Ayam Kam Heong.
MFM said the products were developed with an emphasis on convenience, food safety and nutritional accessibility, while also aligning with clean-label trends and international regulatory standards.
The group said the RTE portfolio is intended for both domestic and export markets as Malaysia seeks to strengthen its position as a global halal food production hub.
As part of its marketing strategy, MFM plans to introduce a reality-based cooking programme titled "Rasa Warrior Showdown" alongside a digital content series featuring its products.
The event also featured a cooking demonstration by celebrity chef Chef Fikree Aznan.
Established in 1966 with Malaysia's first modern flour mill in Lumut, Perak, MFM has since expanded into flour milling, poultry integration and aquaculture operations across Malaysia, Vietnam and Indonesia.
The group currently has a combined daily wheat milling capacity of about 10,000 metric tonnes, while its Vietnam operations produce more than 700,000 tonnes of flour annually.
MFM said its poultry business uses Industry 4.0 technologies, including climate control and precision farming systems, to improve operational efficiency and maintain animal welfare standards.
Through its joint venture with Tyson Foods under Dindings Tyson Sdn Bhd, the group said it maintains a production capacity of up to 400,000 birds daily.
The company added that feed grain supply is supported through Premier Grain Sdn Bhd, a joint venture with Toyota Tsusho Corp, helping to stabilise sourcing amid market volatility.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asian currencies rise, stocks hit record high on Iran peace deal hopes
Article:
HONG KONG: Asian currencies gained, and stocks rallied to a record high on Thursday, buoyed by hopes that the Middle East war would end as Iran reviewed a US peace proposal, sending oil prices lower and fuelling risk plays.
US President Donald Trump expected the war in Iran to be over soon as he sought a peace deal with Tehran to end the stalemate and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which channels a fifth of global oil supplies.
Oil prices fell to two-week lows on Wednesday, but rose about US$1 in Asian trade on Thursday. The MSCI's gauge of emerging Asian stocks hit a record high and is up 23.4 per cent this year so far.
Taiwan climbed 2.5 per cent to an all-time high, bringing 2026 gains to more than 45 per cent, as investors shifted focus to rallying chip stocks while worries over the conflict eased.
In the Philippines, stocks added as much as 2.3 per cent to hit their strongest level since April 10, providing some respite to the underperforming equities.
The benchmark posted losses in three of the previous four years. Indonesian stocks added over 1 per cent, up for a fourth straight session.
Thai and Singapore stocks rose 0.2 per cent each. Currencies also gained ground. The Taiwan dollar strengthened as much as 0.5 per cent to 31.338, its highest level since early March.
The Singapore dollar gained 0.1 per cent and is up 1.45 per cent this year, outperforming most Asian currencies. "Further de-escalation in the Middle East, such as Iran accepting the US proposed deal and a gradual reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, could continue to support gains in Asian forex," analysts at MUFG said in a note.
The Malaysian ringgit was up 0.3 per cent at 3.9080 per US dollar. The country's central bank will release its monetary policy decision later in the day and is widely expected to stand pat on rates, keeping them unchanged through the rest of the year.
The Malaysian economy has been largely resilient despite the Middle East conflict, clocking benign inflation and steady growth. The Philippine peso eased 0.1 per cent, and the Thai baht fell 0.4 per cent.
The currencies are down 3.2 per cent and 2.7 per cent this year, respectively, amid higher oil prices that have fueled inflation. The Middle East conflict caused inflation in bothcountries to jump to three-year highs. Prices in South Korea rose to a near two-year high. The higher oil bill is beginning to weigh on growth as well.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM3.95mil contraband cigarettes and alcohol seized in Penang raids
Article:
PENANG: The Customs Department has dealt a major blow to a smuggling syndicate following two separate raids in Seberang Prai last month, seizing contraband cigarettes and alcoholic beverages worth RM3.95 million, including unpaid duties and taxes.
The operations, carried out in Tasek Gelugor and Bukit Mertajam, uncovered hundreds of thousands of illicit cigarette sticks and more than 63,000 litres of alcohol believed to be destined for the local black market.
Acting state Customs director Ramizah Bee Salleh Ghany said the first raid was conducted on April 22 after enforcement officers acted on intelligence information.
She said a team from the Penang Customs Enforcement Division raided a shop premises in Tasek Gelugor, suspected of being used as a storage facility for smuggled cigarettes.
"Inspection of the premises uncovered various brands of white and kretek cigarettes concealed inside a toilet and beneath piles of used boxes.
"A local man in his late 30s, believed to be the owner of the premises, was detained to assist in investigations," she told newsmen at the Bagan Jermal enforcement store today.
Customs seized 242,640 sticks of cigarettes in the raid, with the total value of the confiscated goods, including duties and taxes, estimated at RM263,233.08.
Ramizah said the syndicate used hidden storage compartments within the premises to avoid detection by authorities.
In a second operation on April 24, Customs officers raided an unoccupied warehouse in Mukim 19, Telok Wang, Bukit Mertajam following a public tip-off.
The raid uncovered 130,800 sticks of illicit cigarettes and 63,674.30 litres of beer and liquor of various brands suspected to be untaxed goods.
"The total value of the seizure in the second case, including duties and taxes, is estimated at RM3,681,848.56.
"All the cigarettes and alcoholic beverages are believed to have been intended for distribution in the local market," she added.
She said the syndicate was believed to have used the abandoned warehouse as a transit and storage point to conceal the contraband before it was distributed.
No arrests were made in the second raid, while both cases remain under investigation.
The cases are being investigated under Section 135(1)(d) of the Customs Act 1967 for knowingly possessing, storing, concealing or harbouring uncustomed or prohibited goods.
Upon conviction, offenders may face fines of between 10 and 20 times the value of the seized goods, or between RM100,000 and RM500,000, whichever is higher, imprisonment of between six months and five years, or both.
Ramizah said cigarettes and alcoholic beverages are classified as prohibited imports under the Customs (Prohibition of Imports) Order 2023.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia ready to absorb 850 NUMed students affected by UK policy shift
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is prepared to accommodate 850 Newcastle University Medicine Malaysia (NUMed) students affected by changes to the United Kingdom's medical training policy, allowing them to complete their housemanship locally.
Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad assured the students that they would not be left stranded despite the policy shift in the UK.
"I want to assure the 850 NUMed students affected that they will not be stranded. Although the UK has changed its policy, the Health Ministry is ready to absorb them for housemanship training at our 48 training hospitals nationwide," he told reporters after officiating the opening of the CRM Trial Connect Conference 2026 here.
Dzulkefly said the Medical Development Division had confirmed there were no objections to the students undergoing training in Malaysia using existing modules, subject to current entry requirements.
For Malaysian citizens, applications may be submitted through SPA9, with applicants required to fulfil the conditions of the service scheme, including obtaining a pass in Bahasa Melayu at SPM level.
"For non-citizens, including spouses and permanent residents, applications may be submitted through the Health Ministry's Contract Officer Selection Committee before endorsement by the Public Service Department and the Public Service Commission.
"We have the capacity, and we want your talent to serve our people," he said.
He acknowledged the concerns among students over the policy changes in the UK, but stressed that Malaysia remained committed to supporting its medical graduates.
"I understand the disappointment caused by the policy changes in the UK. However, my priority as Health Minister is to ensure our medical talent has a place to develop.
"If the UK closes its doors, Malaysia will always open its doors for you to return and serve," he added.
However, Dzulkefly said any arrangement must comply with Malaysia's existing regulatory framework governing medical registration and training.
"The registration, training and professional qualification processes are subject to statutory requirements and regulations set by the Malaysian Medical Council (MMC) under the Medical Act 1971.
"The NUMed degree remains recognised under the Medical Act 1971," he said.
He said that further engagement might be held with the university and relevant stakeholders, including counterparts in the UK, depending on future developments.
"We will look into this matter further and follow up accordingly through the MMC," he added.
About 850 Malaysian medical students at NUMed are reportedly facing uncertainty following the implementation of the UK Medical Training (Preference) Act 2026, which changes entry requirements for the Foundation Programme (FY1/FY2).
People's Progressive Party president Datuk Dr Loga Bala Mohan said the policy change meant that NUMed students would no longer receive priority for training placements under the UK National Health Service (NHS).
He said the new rules prioritised graduates who complete both their studies and clinical training in the UK, placing NUMed graduates – who study in Malaysia – in the category of international medical graduates (IMGs).
UK Health Secretary Wes Streeting, who introduced the Bill in the House of Commons in January, previously said the NHS should not "poach" doctors from countries already facing shortages while local graduates struggle to secure training placements.
The law establishes a priority system for entry into two key pathways in UK medical practice – the UK Foundation Programme (UKFP) and specialty training.
The UKFP is a compulsory two-year training programme that bridges medical school and full registration with the General Medical Council (GMC). Without completing the programme, doctors cannot practise independently in the UK.
According to the British Medical Association, UKFP placements under the Act must first be offered to UK medical graduates and designated priority groups before other eligible applicants are considered.
The priority groups include graduates from Ireland, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway and Switzerland.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 20km police chase suspect claims trial to 14 charges
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A coffee shop worker who was arrested days after a dramatic 20km police chase that allegedly left 12 vehicles damaged was charged with 14 offences at two separate magistrates' courts here today.
S. Prasaanndh, 29, pleaded not guilty to all charges after they were read before magistrates Nadratun Naim Mohd Saidi and Nur Fadrina Zulkhairi.
The offences include obstructing a police officer, reckless driving and committing mischief by damaging multiple vehicles during the incident on April 30.
Twelve charges were framed under Section 427 of the Penal Code, while the remaining charges were under Section 186 of the Penal Code and Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
Section 427 pertains to mischief causing damage to property amounting to RM25 or more, punishable by up to five years' imprisonment, a fine, or both upon conviction. Section 186 concerns obstructing a public servant from carrying out official duties, while Section 42(1) relates to reckless and dangerous driving.
At Nadratun's court, Prasaanndh faced five charges, including allegedly obstructing a police corporal from carrying out his duties at Jalan Tanjung Bungah at about 9am on April 30.
He was also accused of driving recklessly from Jalan Persiaran Gurney to Jalan Tengah in Bayan Baru around the same time.
The remaining charges involved allegedly damaging three vehicles – a Perodua Alza, an Isuzu pickup truck and a Toyota Alphard – with losses estimated between RM700 and RM1,000.
At Nur Fadrina's court, he faced nine additional charges of damaging nine other vehicles, including a Perodua Myvi, Volkswagen, Jeep, Hyundai, Toyota Yaris and Proton X70, among others.
The alleged incidents took place at several locations, including Jalan Sungai Pinang, Jalan Burma and Lebuhraya Tun Dr Lim Chong Eu between 9am and 10.20am.
Deputy Public Prosecutors Ahmad Danial Amir Saad and Lau Shavin prosecuted, while the accused was represented by lawyer Sharmila Shanthini Mogan.
At Nadratun's court, Ahmad Danial asked for bail to be set at RM6,000 for the first count, RM10,000 for the second count and RM6,000 for each of the subsequent three counts.
Sharmila appealed for a minimal bail, saying her client works at a coffee shop, has no fixed monthly income and supports his wife and seven-year-old child.
She also told the court that her client has a permanent job and address in Penang, and is not a flight risk.
Nadratun then set bail at RM26,000 and fixed June 15 for mention.
At Nur Fadrina's court, Lau sought RM63,000 bail in total for the nine charges, citing the seriousness of the offences.
However, Nur Fadrina set bail at RM30,000 and also fixed June 15 for mention.
Police previously said
the suspect was arrested
at a house in Gelugor about 8.30pm on Sunday following a manhunt launched after the April 30 incident.
The chase allegedly began after police spotted a suspicious vehicle along Green Lane. When ordered to stop, the driver allegedly sped off towards Persiaran Gurney, ramming several vehicles before escaping after a pursuit spanning about 20km.
A video of the pursuit later went viral on social media.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's services PPI up 2.1pct in Q1: Statistics Department
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's services producer price index (PPI) rose 2.1 per cent in the first quarter of 2026 (Q1 2026), compared with 1.0 per cent recorded in Q4 2025, according to the Statistics Department.
In a statement today, chief statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the increase was mainly driven by higher accommodation and food and beverage (F&B) service activities, transportation, and education.
"The accommodation and F&B service activities index rose 5.7 per cent, compared with 3.1 per cent in the previous quarter, driven by higher restaurant and mobile food service activities index (5.2 per cent) and beverage service activities (3.1 per cent).
"The transportation index increased 3.8 per cent from 2.2 per cent in Q4 2025, mainly due to a 6.4 per cent rise in the passenger air transport index," he said.
On education, Mohd Uzir said the index increased 1.9 per cent (Q4 2025: 1.5 per cent), supported by secondary education (1.7 per cent) and higher education (1.5 per cent).
"Other indices that registered increases were arts, entertainment and recreation (1.6 per cent), health (1.5 per cent), professional services (1.0 per cent) and real estate activities (0.6 per cent)," he said.
He added that the information and communication index recorded a marginal increase of 0.1 per cent, rebounding from a 0.1 per cent decline in Q4 2025, mainly due to the computer programming, consultancy and related activities index (0.8 per cent).
Mohd Uzir further said that on a quarter-on-quarter basis, the services PPI increased 1.4 per cent, compared with a 0.3 per cent increase in Q4 2025.
"This increase was mainly contributed by the arts, entertainment and recreation index (6.3 per cent), and accommodation and food and beverage service activities (3.5 per cent).
"Other contributing sectors were transportation (1.6 per cent), education (1.1 per cent), health (1.0 per cent) and professional services (0.8 per cent), while the real estate activities and information and communication indices recorded marginal increases of 0.4 per cent and 0.2 per cent, respectively," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia to strengthen elderly care through new legislation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is mulling introducing a new law on parental care aimed at making the care of elderly parents a responsibility of their children.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the Women, Family and Community Development Ministry has been directed to draft and present the legislation to the cabinet within the next six months.
Speaking at the launch of the National Social Policy Action Plan 2026–2030 today, Zahid said the proposed law could draw on examples from countries that have already implemented similar legislation.
"This is a new act concerning the care of parents, which will become the responsibility of their children.
"Several countries have already implemented similar laws, but our country is still at the research and study stage.
"I have requested that the ministry submit a proposal for the drafting of the act within six months and it must then be presented to the cabinet for policy approval," he told reporters after the event.
Present were Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri, her deputy Lim Hui Ying and Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad.
Zahid said Malaysia is beginning to witness increasing neglect of elderly parents, including cases where they are placed in nursing homes and left without visits from family members.
He described such actions as "an abandonment of responsibility that is unacceptable".
"We do not want Malaysia, as it progresses into a developed nation, to neglect social responsibility towards parents," he said.
He said religious and Eastern values must not be eroded or ignored, stressing the need to balance worldly and spiritual responsibilities.
"All races and religions emphasise the importance of caring for parents, although the approach may differ from one country to another.
"However, Malaysia has its own distinct values, and these important principles must continue to be strengthened.
"We must learn from the experiences of countries that have already implemented such laws.
"And if there are countries that have yet to do so, we believe they may eventually move in the same direction, especially as social awareness and interfaith understanding are further enhanced," he said.
Meanwhile, on the National Social Policy Action Plan, he said it must serve as a bridge between government policies and the lives of the people.
He said the plan must ensure assistance does not overlap, information is not interrupted, vulnerable groups are not left out and every intervention truly reaches those in need.
"A strong society is not built programme by programme, but household by household. Therefore, our shift must be clear from policy to action, from programme to solutions and from assistance to empowerment.
"Because ultimately, social policy is only valuable when it can change the lives of the people," he said.
Driven by four main thrusts, the National Social Policy Action Plan covers 102 initiatives.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wasco Greenergy installs one of Malaysia's largest biomass steam systems
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Wasco Greenergy Bhd has installed and commissioned one of Malaysia's largest biomass steam energy systems at a paper manufacturing facility in Kajang, Selangor.
The company said the project supports the industry's transition towards more efficient and sustainable energy solutions.
Designed and manufactured by subsidiary Wasco Thermal Sdn Bhd, the system is built for high-efficiency power generation at scale.
Fuelled mainly by treated empty fruit bunches, the system is designed to achieve a utilisation rate of more than 80 per cent, reducing reliance on conventional fuels and the national grid.
Wasco Greenergy said the system uses a cogeneration setup to produce both renewable electricity and steam energy, improving efficiency and operational reliability.
The automated system is also designed to maintain consistent performance under varying operating conditions at the paper mill.
The company said the system is expected to generate up to 500,000 megawatt-hours of renewable thermal energy annually.
This helps to reduce carbon dioxide emissions and improve long-term energy planning and operational efficiency.
It added that the project could also create opportunities for carbon credit generation, supporting low-carbon industrial operations.
Chief executive officer Lee Yee Chong said the project proves that large-scale biomass energy is not just environmentally sound, but also commercially compelling.
"By empowering industries to convert agricultural waste into a reliable energy source, we are supporting Malaysia's ambitions under the National Energy Transition Roadmap and aligning with the objectives of the National Biomass Action Plan," he said in a statement.
Through its engineering, procurement, construction, and commissioning services and innovative build-own-operate models, Wasco Greenergy provides industrial clients with flexible pathways to adopt renewable energy.
The project has contributed to the company's financial performance for the past two years.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DBKL steps up flood mitigation plan
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is preparing short, medium and long-term measures to reduce flash floods in high-risk areas following heavy rain in several parts of the city yesterday.
Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud said heavy rain in several areas, especially Segambut and Cheras, caused rapid surface runoff from higher ground, with drains along roads and in low-lying areas unable to cope with the volume of water during the downpour.
"During the heavy rain, surface runoff was very fast, especially along roads.
"At that time, the drains could not cope. We have set aside an allocation this year to improve drainage in all areas, including Cheras," he said at a press conference at Taman Lemak here today.
Also present were Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh and Cheras MP Tan Kok Wai.
Fadlun said he had asked Tan to work with DBKL on measures to reduce pressure on flood-prone low-lying areas.
For Segambut, he said DBKL had identified a site for land acquisition to build an on-site detention pond to temporarily hold rainwater before releasing it into rivers, easing pressure on the drainage system during heavy rain.
Fadlun said DBKL had yet to receive precise rainfall intensity data from the Malaysian Meteorological Department, but yesterday's floods subsided soon after the rain stopped.
"This means the drainage system was functioning. But during heavy rain, its capacity is strained, especially in low-lying areas which receive runoff from higher ground. These are the areas affected by flash floods," he said.
Fadlun said DBKL was also tightening controls on hillside and slope developments after land clearing in areas such as Taman Tun Dr Ismail increased surface runoff, causing rainwater to flow directly into drainage systems instead of being absorbed by trees and soil.
He said DBKL would no longer allow development sites on slopes and hillsides to be opened all at once, adding that such projects must be carried out in phases.
"For all construction areas, I have also asked DBKL to enforce maintenance of ponds, silt fences and silt traps so water at development sites can be properly contained before flowing into nearby areas," he said.
Meanwhile, Yeoh said DBKL had begun daily weather announcements through radio stations from May 6, using MetMalaysia data, to alert city residents and help them take early precautions.
She said the year-long initiative would help residents plan ahead, including avoiding parking in low-lying areas during expected heavy rain.
Yesterday's heavy rain triggered flash floods in several parts of the Klang Valley, including Kuala Lumpur and Petaling Jaya, causing traffic congestion and leaving several vehicles stranded.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: Malaysia mulling ‘filial responsibility’ law to ensure children care for ageing parents
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 7 — The government is considering a new law that would legally obligate children to care for their ageing parents, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today.
Speaking to reporters after launching the National Social Policy Action Plan 2026-2030, Zahid said the proposed legislation aims to introduce a legal framework placing clearer emphasis on filial responsibility.
“The proposed law will focus on the responsibility of children in caring for their parents,” he said.
“Several countries have already implemented similar legislation, but Malaysia is still at the stage of discussion and review.”
Zahid has instructed the Ministry of Women, Family, and Community Development to prepare a policy paper on the proposed Act within six months.
The paper will then be presented to the Cabinet for policy approval before any legislative drafting begins.
The deputy prime minister stressed that the move is crucial to ensure that Malaysia’s social and moral fabric remains intact as it progresses toward developed nation status.
He also warned against the erosion of religious and cultural values, stressing the need to balance material development with spiritual and moral obligations.
“All races and religions place importance on the duty of caring for one’s parents, although different countries may adopt different approaches,” Zahid added.
“Malaysia has its own social values and identity that must be preserved and strengthened.”
He confirmed that the government will study legal models from other countries that have enacted similar "filial responsibility" laws.
However, he gave his assurance that any framework developed for Malaysia will be carefully aligned with local culture, religious sensitivities, and social conditions.

Title: Selangor Domestic Trade Ministry foils LPG misappropriation, seizes 250 cylinders in Kuala Langat estate
Article:
SHAH ALAM, May 7 — The Selangor Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) has dismantled a syndicate involved in the misappropriation of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG), operating from unnumbered premises in an oil palm estate in Tanjung Sepat, Kuala Langat, and detained two Indonesian men in their 20s.
Its director Muhamad Hanif Asa’ari said the suspects were caught transferring LPG from subsidised 14-kilogramme cylinders into 50-kg industrial cylinders during the Ops Tiris 4.0 raid conducted with the police at about 3 pm yesterday, following a month-long intelligence operation.
He said in a statement today that initial investigations found the premises had been operating for about a month, supplying LPG to industrial buyers at below-market prices.
“The syndicate obtained subsidised LPG supplies from licensed wholesalers in Banting, Tanjung Sepat and Sungai Pelek to avoid detection by the authorities.
“The ministry is now focusing on identifying the supply chain that enabled the syndicate to obtain large quantities of subsidised LPG for illegal activities,” he said.
Also seized were 250 LPG cylinders of various brands, a platform weighing scale, and other equipment, with the total seizure estimated at RM400,000.
Muhamad Hanif said the operation prevented an estimated RM750,000 in subsidy leakages.
“Investigations are being conducted under the Control of Supplies Act 1961, particularly Section 21, to identify the owner of the premises and the company believed to be involved in the activity.
“The two Indonesian suspects have been taken in for questioning to assist investigations before prosecution proceedings are initiated,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Zahid: Umno not rejecting Aminuddin outright, but he should not interfere in Negeri Sembilan royal affairs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 7 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has clarified that the party is not outright rejecting Negeri Sembilan MB Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, but instead withholding support over concerns related to alleged interference in Negeri Sembilan’s customary laws.
Zahid said the position was not limited to Umno Negeri Sembilan alone, but also reflected the party’s broader stance.
“We are not rejecting him, but we are not supporting him as long as he is interfering in Negeri Sembilan’s
adat
laws,” he told reporters after officiating the National Social Policy Action Plan 2026-2030 here today.
“We also want matters concerning the traditional rulers of Negeri Sembilan, namely the Yang di-Pertuan to be respected, and for those issues to be resolved by the relevant parties themselves,” he added.
This comes after Umno Negeri Sembilan issued a statement expressing support for the unity government while at the same time rejecting Aminuddin.
MORE TO COME

Title: Health minister: Malaysia must strengthen clinical research ecosystem to become global contributor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 7 — Malaysia must move beyond being merely a recipient or a conduit of external innovation by shaping its own unique footprint on the global map of medical discovery, says Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad.
He said that, to place Malaysia on the global map of medical discovery, the country must empower its human capital, intensify and expand collaborative networks, and strengthen a sustainable and competitive ecosystem across all aspects of research.
“I believe this transition from being a consumer of science to becoming a global contributor is integral to Malaysia’s journey towards becoming a Global Trusted Research Nation.
“To keep pace with our ambition, we must continue strengthening our site resources by improving facilities, operational systems and essential research support such as our study coordinators. Strong infrastructure is not optional; it is the foundation of scientific integrity,” he said in his speech at the launch of the 5th Annual Clinical Research Malaysia (CRM) Trial Connect Conference 2026 here today.
Also present were Dr Akhmal Yusof, chief executive officer of Clinical Research Malaysia; Dr Yasuhiro Fujiwara, chief executive of the Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency; and Dr Timothy Yap, medical oncologist and physician-scientist at the University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Centre.
Meanwhile, Dzulkefly said Malaysia’s current clinical research ecosystem is powered by more than 3,200 skilled professionals across the industry, reflecting a workforce that is the lifeblood of the country’s progress in the field.
He highlighted that 30 per cent of the principal investigators named in Malaysia’s Roll of Honour last year were newly appointed, describing it as a sign of successful succession planning and strong institutional support.
Dzulkefly also stressed that collaboration was essential in building a resilient and world-class clinical research ecosystem, particularly in these times of geopolitical uncertainties.
He added that CRM had contributed significantly to the country’s Gross National Income (GNI) while creating high-value jobs annually.
The conference, themed “The Rise of Asia in Clinical Trials”, gathered more than 1,100 attendees representing over 150 organisations from 20 countries, including clinical research leaders, international organisations and national agencies across Southeast Asia and the wider Asian region.
